name: pytest-addopts-silently-wins-over-bare-cli
tags: pytest, addopts, marker, integration test, deploy.sh, full suite, silent skip, CLI override
body:
A bare `pytest tests/ -q` with no explicit `-m` flag does NOT override a marker filter baked into `pyproject.toml`'s `addopts` (e.g. `addopts = "-m 'not integration' -n auto"`). Pytest merges CLI args with addopts rather than replacing them — an absent `-m` on the CLI means addopts' `-m` silently wins, with zero warning or error.

**Why:** Cost real production confidence — `scripts/deploy.sh`'s step 5 was labeled "Full suite (unit + integration)" and used a bare `pytest tests/ -q`, but every prior `/deploy` run had silently only executed the 569 unit tests, never the 63 integration tests, while reporting "passed" and shipping to main. Discovered 2026-07-05 (task:cf2f3c5f) only by noticing the test count in the "full suite" step exactly matched the dev worktree's explicit `-m "not integration"` unit-only count.

**How to apply:** Any script/doc that claims to run a "full" or "all tests" suite in a repo with an addopts marker filter must explicitly pass a marker expression that cancels it out (e.g. `-m "integration or not integration"`), never rely on a bare invocation. When auditing a CI/deploy script's test step, check `pyproject.toml`'s `[tool.pytest.ini_options].addopts` first — a suspiciously-matching test count between two "different" test runs is a strong signal one of them isn't running what it claims.
